title: Where do I find the URL of a board in the desktop version?

posts.author: binsento (Vincent)
posts.cookedHtml:
I work in the desktop version since and I can’t find the URL of a board.
Maybe I’m looking in the wrong place?
I sometimes like to drop the URL in a slack channel for people to see and easily discuss something, but I keep having to switch to the browser version, copy the url and close it again.

posts.author: simon_blinno (Simon Blickenstorfer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi binsento,

In the desktop version you can simply copy the URL in the browser (here as an example Google Chrome) and share it in the Slack Channel with your work colleagues.

image772×172 15.7 KB

I hope this solves your problem!

Best regards
Simon

Blinno
monday.com-Partner Switzerland
monday-schweiz.ch

posts.cookedHtml:

In the desktop version there is no address bar:

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
binsento:

In the desktop version there is no address bar:

When I originally read your post, I assumed you were referring to the browser-based version.

However, I suspect you mean the desktop app. Is that right?

Support

monday.com's desktop app

You may have asked yourself, "I wonder if monday.com has a desktop app?" Well, we definitely understand this need, so yes, we do have one! We offer a desktop app for both Mac and Window computers. ...

posts.author: simon_blinno (Simon Blickenstorfer)
posts.cookedHtml:
The only way I’ve found is to go to the developer tools (CTRL+ALT+I) and then search (CTRL+F) for your monday url for example company.monday.com Then you can copy the URL. It’s working, but it’s not the easiest way.

image513×735 26.7 KB

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @binsento!

The easiest way to find a board URL is to open up one of the items on the board and select the three dot menu in the corner of the item view.
In this menu you’ll see an option to copy the item link:

image1368×768 90 KB

If you then paste the item link, you can simply remove the /pulse section of the URL to get the board URL, like in the example below:

image1988×294 65.2 KB

Alternatively, you may find it easier to revert to the web browser version when you need to retrieve a board ID

Best,

Dani
